reclassifications.reason: The core data service is the business logic layer consumed by the data route. Static analysis likely missed this because the re-export barrel (src/core/index.ts or similar) is new. It is a direct dependency of the data route and belongs in the same review group.

This barrel re-export file is part of the 'feature/reexport' refactor. The adapter layer sits between the route and the core service; static analysis failed to trace reachability through the new index re-export. It logically belongs with the data route change.

This facade barrel re-export is part of the same re-export restructuring as the other index files. It is likely the direct import surface used by src/routes/data.ts and should be reviewed alongside the route and the layers it re-exports.

response.reasoning:
Looking at the diff, the branch is named "feature/reexport" which strongly suggests this is a re-export/module structure refactoring. The three ungrouped files (src/adapter/index.ts, src/core/dataService.ts, src/facade/index.ts) are classic re-export barrel files that static analysis failed to trace from the entrypoint. However, src/routes/data.ts likely imports from these modules (adapter, core service, or facade), so they are logically connected to the route change.

The branch name "feature/reexport" and the file names (index.ts barrel files + a core service) suggest this PR is introducing or modifying re-export patterns across the adapter, core, and facade layers. The route file (src/routes/data.ts) is likely consuming these exports. All four files appear to be part of the same logical change: restructuring module exports and updating the data route to use them.

I should reclassify all three ungrouped files into group_1 since they are logically part of the same data service/route change. The route likely depends on the adapter, facade, and core data service - and static analysis simply failed to trace the reachability path (possibly because the index.ts files are new barrel re-exports that weren't previously in the symbol graph).
